title: The Tale of Jemima Puddle-Duck (excerpt)
author: Beatrix Potter
note: Verbatim opening — from 'What a funny sight…' through Jemima reaching the wood ('…a safe quiet spot.'), one short paragraph per spread. Trope is 'departure' (the naïve protagonist setting out), matching what the EXCERPT contains: the full tale is cautionary, but the foxy-whiskered gentleman, the peril, and the lesson all come after this cut — so, like ugly-duckling, the trope reflects the excerpt, not the whole work. A second picture-band Potter narrative alongside peter-rabbit, comparable scale (~155 vs ~185 words).
